title: KISS transport: SetHardware response uses hardcoded port 0
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/kiss_transport.c
Lines: 261-276

In handle_sethardware(), the response frame is always encoded with port 0:

    if (kiss_encode(0, resp_cmd, response, (size_t)resp_len,
                    resp_frame, sizeof(resp_frame), &resp_frame_len) == 0) {

However, SetHardware commands could arrive on any port (the cmd byte in dispatch_frame contains port info at line 281). The response should go back on the same port the request came from.

Per KISS protocol, while SetHardware is typically port-agnostic, responses should maintain port consistency for applications that use port multiplexing.

Fix: Pass the original request port to handle_sethardware() and use it in the response encoding.
